user_query:
Hey! Can you help me do a quick USB risk assessment for our shared field laptop that we take on hikes? I included a small simulator so we don’t have to plug in anything for real.

Please do the following using only the files in this workspace and local commands:

1) Run the simulator and capture its outputs:
- Execute: python3 input/scan_simulator.py, and save standard output to logs/scan_stdout.txt and standard error to logs/scan_stderr.txt (create the logs/ folder if needed). The analysis must be based on these captured outputs.

2) Build a structured risk report by combining the captured outputs with the allowed devices list:
- Allowed devices are defined by vendor_id + product_id in input/allowed_usb_devices.csv.
- From logs/scan_stdout.txt, parse each line starting with "DEVICE:" into fields (name, vendor_id, product_id, serial, class) and detect whether "INFO: Auto-mount is enabled" appears.
- From logs/scan_stderr.txt, collect lines that begin with WARNING, ERROR, or CRITICAL and associate them to devices by matching the device name or the vendor_id/product_id mentioned in the message.
- A device is allowed if its vendor_id and product_id pair exists in input/allowed_usb_devices.csv; otherwise it is unrecognized.
- Risk levels per device:
  - high if the device has any associated CRITICAL message, or if it is unrecognized
  - medium if no CRITICAL but at least one WARNING
  - low otherwise
- Create outputs/usb_risk_findings.json with:
  - devices: an array where each item has name, vendor_id, product_id, device_class, allowed (boolean), issues (list of associated WARNING/ERROR/CRITICAL messages), and risk_level ("low"|"medium"|"high").
  - overall_summary: total_devices, unrecognized_devices, critical_findings (count of CRITICAL lines), warnings_count (count of WARNING lines), errors_count (count of ERROR lines), and auto_mount_enabled (boolean based on stdout).

3) Update the existing checklist with mitigations:
- Append a new section at the end of docs/field-laptop-checklist.md titled "USB Device Risk Mitigations" that includes actionable bullets driven by your findings:
  - If auto_mount_enabled is true, include a bullet to disable auto-mount for USB mass storage on the field laptop.
  - For each unrecognized device, include a bullet to block that vendor_id/product_id via a udev rule until manually approved.
  - For each device with high risk, include a bullet advising not to use that device (include its name and vendor/product IDs) until investigated.

Deliverables:
- logs/scan_stdout.txt and logs/scan_stderr.txt (from running the simulator)
- outputs/usb_risk_findings.json (as specified)
- Modified docs/field-laptop-checklist.md with the appended "USB Device Risk Mitigations" section

Please keep paths and filenames exactly as above so I can verify the results. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
